title: Frenela columellaris
taxonomy: Frenela columellaris is classified in the family Frenelaceae, which is a group of flowering plants that are often found in tropical regions. This classification makes Frenela columellaris a part of the larger order of plants known as Magnoliales. The family Frenelaceae is also closely related to other plant families such as Annonaceae and Lauraceae. Within the family Frenelaceae, Frenela columellaris is one of the species that is most commonly studied. Further research is needed to fully understand the classification and relationships within the family Frenelaceae.

intro: Frenela columellaris, also known as False Frenkelia or Wild Rue, is a shrubby perennial plant belonging to the family Boraginaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its numerous health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it effective in treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Frenela columellaris include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
